name: City Traders Imperium
description_short: South African prop firm offering funded forex and indices accounts up to $200K through evaluation or direct funding, with profit splits up to 80%.
description_long:
City Traders Imperium (CTI) is a South African-based proprietary trading firm that provides traders with access to funded accounts ranging from $10,000 to $200,000. The firm offers two primary pathways to funding: a traditional evaluation challenge program and a direct funding option, giving traders flexibility to choose the route that best suits their trading style and financial situation.

The evaluation program follows a structured challenge format where traders must hit defined profit targets while adhering to daily and overall drawdown limits. Upon successfully completing the evaluation, traders receive a live funded account with profit splits reaching up to 80%. CTI supports trading via MetaTrader 5 and focuses primarily on forex pairs, major global indices, commodities, and metals.

A distinguishing aspect of City Traders Imperium is its strong emphasis on trader development. The firm operates a trading academy that offers structured courses, mentorship sessions, and community-driven learning. This makes CTI particularly attractive to intermediate traders who want both capital access and continued skill refinement. The educational component includes webinars, video content, and an active online community.

While CTI is a smaller firm compared to industry leaders like FTMO or Topstep, it has cultivated a loyal following among traders who value its transparent rules, educational resources, and community culture. The firm has an active social media presence and engages regularly with its trader base, which contributes to its strong support reputation relative to its size.
pros: Integrated trading academy with courses and mentorship sets it apart from most prop firms
Flexible pathways to funding including direct funding and evaluation options
Profit splits up to 80% are competitive within the industry
Strong community culture with active trader engagement and support
Transparent trading rules with no hidden restrictions
cons: Smaller and less established than top-tier prop firms like FTMO or Topstep
Primarily focused on forex and indices with limited market diversity
No proprietary trading platform or performance dashboard tools
best_for: developing forex traders seeking mentorship and capital
traders who want education alongside funded account access
